hero.title: Building Bridges Beyond Boundaries
hero.subtitle: AUF's partnerships with institutions, industries, and international networks drive innovation, enrich academic programs, and open doors for students and faculty worldwide.

guidelines.title: Guidelines on Establishing Partnership with AUF
guidelines.description: AUF welcomes collaborative relationships with local and international institutions, industries, government agencies, and non-governmental organizations. All partnerships must align with AUF's vision, mission, and institutional priorities, and must undergo the standard partnership establishment process outlined below.

policy.title: AUF Policy for External Affairs
policy.summary: The AUF is guided by its mission, vision, and educational philosophy in setting standards and guidelines for harmonized, effective and efficient implementation of its programs and initiatives for establishing networks, linkages, and partnerships. The AUF Policy on External Affairs guides the university towards achieving its goal on establishing strategic partnerships with external partners to support the holistic development of its students, teaching employee, and non-teaching staff.
principles: Partnerships shall be mutually beneficial and support one or more of the University's three-fold functions: instruction, research, and community engagement.

Partnerships shall align with the University's Vision, Mission, Educational Philosophy, strategic priorities, and institutional values, and shall contribute to enhancing the University's reputation and long-term development.
Partnerships shall adhere to the requirements of applicable laws, regulations, and the policies of relevant regulatory and accrediting bodies.
Partnerships shall undergo the review and approval process established by the University prior to execution and implementation.

Partnerships for internship, practicum, clinical placement, and other academic experiential learning activities shall be reviewed and endorsed by the Office of the Vice President for Academic Affairs (OVPAA).

Partnerships shall be established for a defined period and shall be subject to regular monitoring, evaluation, and renewal based on performance, relevance, and mutual compliance with the agreed terms.

flow.title: Preliminary discussion with the prospective partner
flow.details:
The prospective partner shall be given a venue for a preliminary discussion on the proposed partnership. The AUF shall explain both the national and institutional requirements in terms of forging linkages and partnership.

During the preliminary discussion, the nature and scope of the partnership shall be identified.

Reference: Get in touch with the Target College or Unit.
*Link to CONTACT US

flow.title: Submission of the Proposal
flow.details:
The Prospective Partner, supported by the concerned academic/support unit must accomplish the Partnership Proposal Form (Form: AUF-Form-OVPEA-1).

*Link the Form to https://bit.ly/auf-ovpea1

The accomplished Partnership Proposal Form will be forwarded to the concerned College/Unit for review and to ascertain its feasibility. If the unit has decided that the proposal is feasible and will bring mutual benefits to both parties, the Unit head shall endorse the proposal to the OVPEA.

flow.title: Endorsement of the proposal
flow.details:
The Unit head shall endorse the proposal by accomplishing the endorsement form (Form: AUF-Form-OVPEA-2).

*Link the Form to https://bit.ly/auf-ovpea2

flow.title: Evaluation of the proposal
flow.details: The OVPEA shall review the proposal based on the Partnership Proposal Form and Endorsement Form.

flow.title: Clearance from the Registrar’s Office
flow.details:
If the proposed partnership involves curricular revisions, the OVPEA will seek clearance from the Registrar’s Office to ensure that the proposed program (curriculum) is aligned with the existing relevant policies.

*If no concern with CHED’s PSGs and CMOs, this step may be omitted.

flow.title: Clearance from the OVPAd
flow.office: Office of the Vice President for Administration
flow.details:
If the proposed partnership involves the procurement of new equipment or the construction of a new facility, the Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA) shall seek prior clearance from the Office of the Vice President for Administration (OVPAd) regarding the availability and allocation of the required resources, equipment, and facilities necessary for the implementation of the proposal.

*If the proposed partnership does not involve any facility- or equipment-related requirements, this step may be omitted.

flow.title: Drafting of MOA/MOU
flow.office: Concerned Academic / Support Unit & Prospective Partner
flow.details:
The OVPEA will inform the Concerned Academic / Support Unit to proceed with the drafting of the MOU or MOA.

*To expedite the legal review, and if the prospective partner is amendable, the AUF template can be used. The MOU and MOA template will be forwarded to the concerned college/unit and the prospective partner.

flow.title: Clearance from the OVPAA/OVPRI
flow.office: Office of the VP for Academic Affairs & Office of the VP for Research & Innovation
flow.personInCharge: VP for Academic Affairs & VP for Research & Innovation
flow.details: The drafted MOU/MOA shall be cleared from the OVPAA (Teaching Delivery) and/or OVPRI (Research).

flow.title: Clearance from the OVPF for the relevant fees
flow.office: Office of the Vice President for Finance
flow.details:
If the drafted MOU/MOA involves collection of fees, clearance from the OVPF shall be secured by the Concerned Academic / Support Unit.

*In cases where the proposed partnership involves no monetary component, this step may be omitted.

flow.title: Clearance from the Data Protection Officer
flow.details: To ensure Data Protection of the parties involved, the OVPEA shall seek clearance from the AUF-DPO.

flow.title: Clearance from the legal counsel
flow.details:
The drafted MOU/MOA shall be reviewed and approved by the AUF legal counsel.

*If the drafted MOU/MOA is carried over from the AUF template or previously approved MOU/MOA, this step may be omitted.

flow.title: Signing of the MOA/MOU
flow.office: Concerned Academic/Support Unit & OVPEA & Office of the President
flow.personInCharge: Unit Head & VP for External Affairs & University President
flow.details: The Concerned Academic/Support Unit, OVPEA, and Office of the President shall recommend how the MOA/MOU will be signed.

flow.title: Registration of the MOA/MOU to the IZN Database
flow.details:
The Concerned Academic/Support Unit shall register the established partnership at the University Local and International Partnerships Registry.

Form: AUF-Form-OVPEA-3 (Partnership Registration Form)
*Link the Form to https://bit.ly/auf-ovpea3

guidelines.contactNote: For partnership inquiries, contact the External Affairs Office at connect@auf.edu.ph or (63) (045) 625-2888 local 1783.

forms.title: Links for Partnership Forms
forms.description: Access the official partnership forms and links below. You can click on the bit.ly link or scan the QR code to access the corresponding forms directly.

downloadableForms.name: Partnership Proposal Form
downloadableForms.description: This form is completed by prospective local or international partners to formally present a proposed collaboration with Angeles University Foundation.

downloadableForms.name: Partnership Endorsement Form
downloadableForms.description: This form is completed by the concerned AUF academic or support unit to justify and endorse the proposed partnership.

downloadableForms.name: Partnership Registration Form
downloadableForms.description: This form is used to officially register approved partnerships in the AUF International and Local Partnerships Registry.

downloadableForms.name: Engagement Activity Report
downloadableForms.description: This report documents the implementation, outcomes, and impact of a specific internationalization or partnership-related activity.

downloadableForms.name: Partnership Evaluation Form
downloadableForms.description: This form is completed by external partners to assess the effectiveness, alignment, and sustainability of the collaboration with AUF.

downloadableForms.name: Engagement Request Form
downloadableForms.description: This form is submitted by external institutions seeking approval for a meeting, visit, or engagement activity with AUF.

forms.submissionNote: Submit completed forms to the External Affairs Office, or email them to connect@auf.edu.ph.

networks.title: Networks & Associations
networks.description: AUF is an active member of prestigious international and national academic networks that promote quality assurance, academic excellence, institutional transparency, and cross-border collaboration among universities.

memberships.name: Association of Southeast and East Asian Catholic Colleges and Universities
memberships.description: ASEACCU is a regional association of Catholic higher education institutions in Southeast and East Asia, promoting academic collaboration and spiritual growth in Catholic education.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF benefits from regional Catholic educational exchange, leadership workshops, student camps, and collaborative projects with member universities.

memberships.name: The Association of Universities of Asia and the Pacific
memberships.description: AUAP is a platform for interaction and collaboration among members of higher education institutions in the Asia-Pacific region, promoting regional exchange and development.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF gains access to regional student mobility programs, faculty research fellowships, and institutional leadership networks across the Asia-Pacific.

memberships.name: Association of Catholic Universities of the Philippines
memberships.description: ACUP is a national association of Catholic higher education institutions in the Philippines, working to preserve Catholic identity, promote quality education, and foster cooperation.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF collaborates with Philippine Catholic schools on value-centered educational policies, advocacy, and national academic development.

memberships.name: Catholic Educational Association of the Philippines
memberships.description: CEAP is the national association of Catholic schools in the Philippines, representing thousands of Catholic educational institutions nationwide.
memberships.whatItMeans: Through CEAP, AUF actively participates in national educational policymaking, values education advocacy, and private school welfare representation.

memberships.name: Philippine Accrediting Association of Schools, Colleges and Universities
memberships.description: PAASCU is a voluntary accrediting agency that assesses the quality of academic programs and institutional performance of private schools and universities in the Philippines.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF's accredited programs verify the university's compliance with national quality standards, earning deregulated status and public recognition.

memberships.name: Philippine Association of Colleges and Universities
memberships.description: PACU is a national association representing private colleges and universities in the Philippines, advocating for private education development and university welfare.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF cooperates with private higher education institutions on advocacy, research, capacity building, and lobbying for constructive education policies.

memberships.name: ASEAN University Network-Quality Assurance
memberships.description: AUN-QA is the quality assurance network of the ASEAN University Network (AUN) that coordinates quality assessment and program benchmarking across Southeast Asian universities.
memberships.whatItMeans: AUF's participation in AUN-QA program assessments certifies that its curriculum, teaching, and learning outcomes match international standards.
